features.label: Connection transport (ICE / DTLS)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Pass already_AddRefed types by-value instead of by rvalue reference, in dom/{ipc,mathml,media,notification,streams}.

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 22: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in webrtc jsapi, sdp and logging bridges.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: remove unused macros from r_macros.h

patches.subject: remove unused NR_reg_set_bytes and NR_reg_set2_string.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use 'MakeUnique' for allocation (instead of 'new') for UniquePtr-typed variables, in dom/media/webrtc/.

patches.author: Manuel Bucher
patches.subject: Move originAttributes to socket base class nsASocketHandler

patches.author: Anna Weine
patches.subject: Implement RTCTransportStats.tlsVersion/dtlsCipher/srtpCipher

patches.subject: implement (some of) RTCTransportStats

features.summary: Recent changes in Firefox's connection transport area have focused on enhancing the WebRTC experience, which is the technology behind real-time communication, like video calls. The updates now provide more detailed statistics about the transport layer used in these connections, specifically implementing features to report the TLS version, DTLS cipher, and SRTP cipher being utilized. This improvement not only enhances debugging capabilities but also aids developers in optimizing secure connections for their applications. Additionally, some internal code refinements, such as removing obsolete elements and adopting modern memory management practices, contribute to a cleaner and more efficient codebase, ensuring smoother and more reliable performance for users. **Overall, these updates lay the groundwork for enhanced security and efficiency in real-time communication over Firefox.**

features.label: WebRTC web API (RTCPeerConnection)

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Remove the cache-clearing side effect of TS_NONE.

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Fire closed updates for transports that are torn down, even if they were never active.

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: fix webrtc-stats transportId references

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use 'std::make_unique' for allocation (instead of 'new') for std::unique_ptr-typed variables, in dom/media/webrtc/.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use MakeRefPtr to do allocation for more RefPtr variables in dom/media/webrtc/.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use MakeRefPtr to do allocation for various RefPtr variables in dom/media/webrtc/.

patches.author: Jeff Muizelaar
patches.subject: Enable transport-cc for audio codecs;

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication) API, specifically affecting the RTCPeerConnection functionality, focus on improving the efficiency and reliability of connections. By addressing issues like the removal of cache-clearing side effects and ensuring that closed updates are fired for transports that have been torn down, these changes enhance the handling of communication sessions. Additionally, updates to improve memory management, such as using `std::make_unique` and `MakeRefPtr` for better resource allocation, contribute to a more stable and performant experience. Most importantly, the **_enablement of transport-cc for audio codecs_** allows for improved control over audio transmission, adapting to network conditions in real-time, which is crucial for maintaining call quality in variable network environments.

features.label: libwebrtc integration

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 26: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in gmp.

patches.author: az
patches.subject: Part 6: Integrate H.264 FMTP parser usage into Media Capabilities and WebrtcGmpVideoCodec support functions

patches.author: az
patches.subject: Part 2: Improve accuracy of Media Capabilities support queries based on media resolution/framerate. Uses PDM/PEMFactory checks to more easily allow additional verification in the future.

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Remove the CallBasicStats override.

patches.author: Chun-Min Chang
patches.subject: Map webrtc::H264Level to H264_LEVEL by enumerator name

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc integration in Firefox have enhanced how the browser handles video streaming and communication via WebRTC, the technology behind video calls. Notably, improvements have been made to the integration of H.264 video format support, which is widely used for its efficiency in compressing video. This includes the implementation of an H.264 FMTP (Format Parameter) parser, which helps the browser more accurately determine the capabilities of video streams based on their resolution and framerate. With these changes, Firefox can better manage video quality and performance, creating a smoother experience for users without any visible changes in the current interface. Furthermore, logging improvements through the *MOZ_LOG_FMT* will assist developers in diagnosing issues more effectively, although this will not impact end-user experience directly. Overall, these enhancements are significant for improving video call quality and ensuring compatibility with various media formats.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use MakeUnique instead of 'new' to populate UniquePtrs in dom/media/webrtc/{jsep,sdp}.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Change SdpAttributeList::SetAttribute() and its callers to transfer ownership using UniquePtr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Change SdpAttribute::Clone() to provide the clone in a UniquePtr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: In SdpAttributeList concrete classes, store attributes in UniquePtrs instead of raw pointers.

features.summary: Recent changes to the session negotiation process within Firefox focus on improving memory management related to handling Session Description Protocol (SDP) attributes, which are essential for establishing multimedia sessions like video calls. By using **UniquePtrs**, a smart pointer that automatically manages memory, Firefox enhances safety and efficiency, reducing the risk of memory leaks associated with traditional pointer usage. These modifications mainly involve how SDP attributes are created, stored, and copied within the underlying code, fostering a cleaner and more robust implementation while _not changing any visible user features_.

features.label: WebRTC

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 19: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in webrtc MOZ_LOG-based logs.

patches.author: Anna Weine
patches.subject: Update webrtcdtls.KEA expiration to FF 158

patches.author: az
patches.subject: Part 8: Reject WebRTC MediaCapabilities H.264 queries when resolution/framerate exceeds the requested level

patches.author: az
patches.subject: Part 5: Add H.264 FMTP parser and GTests

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication) technology in Firefox enhance logging and media handling capabilities. By using **MOZ_LOG_FMT** for logging, developers can better track the performance and issues in video calls. Additionally, the expiration for webrtcdtls.KEA has been updated to keep up with security standards, ensuring safer communications. The browser also now rejects H.264 video requests if they exceed specified resolution or frame rate limits, preventing potential playback issues and optimizing performance. Finally, improvements have been made with a new H.264 FMTP (Format Parameter) parser, which aids in managing video streaming formats more effectively.

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to check for missing branch-head commits.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to show branch-head commits for libwebrtc.

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build process introduced two new Python scripts aimed at improving how developers manage their code. One script checks for any missing branch-head commits, helping ensure that all crucial updates and changes are tracked effectively, while the other provides a visual overview of those branch-head commits. These enhancements are significant because they support better collaboration and maintenance of WebRTC, the technology behind video calls, making it easier for engineers to keep the codebase up to date and reduce the risk of issues arising from overlooked changes.

features.label: Android camera capture

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Read camera rotation from a window context so it tracks device rotation on Android.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Android camera capture functionality in Firefox now allow the browser to automatically read the camera's rotation based on the device's orientation. This means that when you turn your device, the camera will adjust accordingly, providing a more intuitive experience when taking photos or recording videos. **This improvement enhances user convenience and ensures that images and videos are correctly aligned, mitigating issues with upside-down or sideways captures.**

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

features.summary: Recent updates to the camera and audio capture features in Firefox have improved the way the browser logs events within the system services that manage these functionalities. Specifically, the integration of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** enhances the logging format to provide clearer and more detailed information for developers working on issues related to capturing audio and video. While this change primarily benefits developers by making debugging easier, it ultimately contributes to a more reliable experience for users engaging in activities like video calls or streaming, as it helps ensure that these features work smoothly and effectively.

features.label: Test camera capture

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Rename GetRealTimeClockRaw, and update a test for a newly-populated stat (was always 0 previously).

features.summary: Recent changes in the camera capture testing area include a renaming of a function to improve clarity and alignment with the latest development standards. Additionally, updates were made to a test related to the real-time clock used in capturing video, which now features a newly populated statistic that was previously returning zero. These adjustments enhance the accuracy and reliability of camera-related functionalities, ensuring better performance for applications utilizing these features, particularly in real-time scenarios.

features.label: WebRTC tests

patches.author: Ryan Curran
patches.subject: Enable crashtest (opt) on macosx1500-aarch64-vms

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC tests have introduced crashtests for the MacOS platform running on Apple’s AArch64 architecture. This enhancement is important because it helps ensure that WebRTC — the technology behind video calls — functions reliably on the latest Mac hardware. **By enabling these crashtests, developers can proactively catch and address potential crashes, improving the overall stability and performance of WebRTC in Firefox.**

features.summary: Recent updates in Firefox's connection transport area enhance the underlying technologies that support features like video calls and real-time communication through WebRTC. Key improvements include the implementation of **RTCTransportStats**, which provides vital metrics about the security aspects of connections, such as the TLS version and encryption ciphers used, helping developers better understand the security performance of their applications. Additionally, code modernization efforts, such as adopting 'MakeUnique' for memory allocation, improve overall efficiency and safety, while the organization of socket attributes streamlines how the browser manages connection information. Collectively, these changes are aimed at ensuring a more secure, efficient, and robust experience for users engaging in real-time communications online.

features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox's integration with **WebRTC**, the technology behind video calls, have focused on enhancing video streaming capabilities and overall performance. Notably, support for the H.264 video format has been improved by integrating a more accurate parser and refining how the browser handles media capabilities based on video resolution and frame rate. These changes also include improved logging for video data, which helps developers better diagnose issues, and a more efficient management of audio bitrate for smoother transmissions. Overall, these enhancements are aimed at ensuring a higher quality video communication experience for users, especially as demand for reliable video calls continues to grow.

features.summary: Recent changes to the WebRTC web API enhance its stability and efficiency, particularly in handling connections. The clearing of the cache side effect of TS_NONE improves how the system manages inactive transport connections, ensuring that closed updates are appropriately communicated, even if those transports were never active. Additionally, enabling transport-cc for audio codecs optimizes how audio quality is monitored and adapted during calls. These updates lead to a more reliable and streamlined experience for users engaging in activities like video conferencing or online gaming, where real-time performance is crucial. **Overall, these improvements contribute to a smoother and more efficient WebRTC experience, ultimately benefiting users relying on this technology for communication.**

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC testing framework in Firefox focused on enhancing compatibility and reliability on macOS platforms. The changes include the re-enabling of several important test types, such as mochitests for screen sharing, which is critical for ensuring the stability of video calls and other real-time communication features. Additionally, crashtests, which help identify when the browser crashes under specific conditions, were re-enabled and optimized. These adjustments are essential to maintain a smooth and reliable experience for users engaging in WebRTC activities, ensuring that technology behind **video calls and online collaboration tools** works seamlessly on newer macOS versions.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's session negotiation component, which is crucial for technologies like WebRTC that enable video calls and real-time communication, have focused on improving memory management and code efficiency. By transitioning to **_UniquePtrs_**, a modern smart pointer in C++, the browser is now better equipped to handle resources automatically without manual memory management, reducing the risk of memory leaks. This ensures that attributes in session descriptions, critical for negotiating media capabilities between browsers, are handled more safely and reliably. As a result, these changes make the underlying codebase cleaner and pave the way for potential enhancements in WebRTC's performance and stability in future releases.

features.label: WebRTC

patches.author: Alastor Wu

features.summary: Recent updates to WebRTC, the technology behind real-time audio and video communication in browsers, introduce several important enhancements. Notably, the system now includes a more robust logging mechanism through **MOZ_LOG**, which will help developers troubleshoot issues more effectively. Additionally, there's an update to the handling of MediaCapabilities, specifically for H.264 video queries; the browser will now reject requests that exceed specified resolution or frame rate limits, ensuring a smoother playback experience without overwhelming the system's resources. Finally, improvements in handling the DTLS (Datagram Transport Layer Security) configuration and the integration of new library support enhance the overall performance and flexibility of WebRTC applications. These changes collectively contribute to a more efficient and reliable user experience with video calls and multimedia streaming on Firefox.

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

patches.subject: Use EventTargetCapability throughout CamerasParent.

patches.subject: Make a HostnameInPref variant usable in workers with a mirror:always StaticPref.

features.summary: Recent updates in the Camera & audio capture area focus on improving how the Firefox browser interacts with system services for better performance and usability. The changes include the integration of **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, which enhances logging capabilities for better troubleshooting and debugging, making it easier to track issues as they arise. Additionally, by implementing **EventTargetCapability** throughout the CamerasParent, the handling of camera events is streamlined, providing a more efficient way for the browser to manage camera access and permissions. Lastly, the introduction of a new variant for hostname preferences ensures that settings related to camera use are effectively applied even in web worker contexts, an important improvement for web applications that rely on background processing without impacting the primary thread.

features.label: Negotiation engine (JSEP)

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Only attempt to associate transceivers when remote m-section is receiving.

patches.author: serge-sans-paille
patches.subject: Activate performance-inefficient-string-concatenation clang-tidy check

features.summary: Recent updates to the negotiation engine, which is part of the JSEP (JavaScript Session Establishment Protocol) used for handling media sessions like video calls, aim to improve its efficiency and performance. The changes include stopping the use of a process that was not suitable for certain application scenarios and refining when transceivers—components that manage media streams—are associated based on the state of the remote media section. These adjustments are significant for enhancing the reliability and responsiveness of real-time communications, ensuring a smoother experience in applications relying on technologies like WebRTC, the backbone of video calling. **Overall, these tweaks stand to boost user experience by making media connections more streamlined and effective.**

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to check for missing branch-head commits.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to show branch-head commits for libwebrtc.

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: updated default_config_env for v149

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build have introduced new Python scripts designed to enhance the management of code versions. One script checks for any missing branch-head commits, ensuring that developers can keep track of the latest updates, while another displays the current branch-head commits for libwebrtc, simplifying version oversight. Additionally, the default configuration environment has been updated for version v149, which helps maintain compatibility with the latest development standards. These changes are important because they improve the efficiency of developers working with **libwebrtc, the underlying technology for video calls**, ensuring a smoother development process and reducing the risk of errors.

features.label: Android camera capture

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Read camera rotation from a window context so it tracks device rotation on Android.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Android camera capture functionality in Firefox now allow the browser to automatically read the camera's rotation based on the device's orientation. This means that when you turn your device, the camera will adjust accordingly, providing a more intuitive experience when taking photos or recording videos. **This improvement enhances user convenience and ensures that images and videos are correctly aligned, mitigating issues with upside-down or sideways captures.**

features.label: Test camera capture

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Rename GetRealTimeClockRaw, and update a test for a newly-populated stat (was always 0 previously).

features.summary: Recent changes in the camera capture testing area include a renaming of a function to improve clarity and alignment with the latest development standards. Additionally, updates were made to a test related to the real-time clock used in capturing video, which now features a newly populated statistic that was previously returning zero. These adjustments enhance the accuracy and reliability of camera-related functionalities, ensuring better performance for applications utilizing these features, particularly in real-time scenarios.
